questions.text: Have cybersecurity risks been globally assessed based on current and emerging threats?
questions.category: Appropiate levels of cybersecurity and consistency (MG01)

questions.text: Are there procedures in place to maintain consistency of security configurations over time?
questions.category: Appropiate levels of cybersecurity and consistency (MG01)

questions.text: Does the system design incorporate mechanisms to detect and respond to unauthorized or anomalous uses?
questions.category: Systems resistant to alterations of use (MG02)

questions.text: Are integrity controls in place to prevent undue modification of system configuration or behavior?
questions.category: Systems resistant to alterations of use (MG02)

questions.text: Have protocols and technical measures been established to detect, report and remedy anomalies or inconsistencies in the dataset?
Have the sources of data collection been mapped, what security controls are in place and who is responsible for monitoring this integrity?
Have the risks of adversarial attacks been identified and assessed at each stage of the system's development?
Does the system incorporate specific detection and mitigation techniques to neutralize potential adversarial attacks in production?
Are adversarial attack exercises conducted on a regular basis to validate resilience to such attacks?
Has it been assessed whether the intelligent system itself may have specific cybersecurity risks?
Have obfuscation, encryption, or component separation techniques been adopted to make it difficult to extract or copy the model?
questions.category: Protection against inversion attacks and/or model theft (MG06)

questions.text: Are robust access controls and authentication in place to prevent unauthorized access to the model?
questions.category: Protection against inversion attacks and/or model theft (MG06)

questions.text: Have response protocols been established for specific incidents of theft or manipulation of the model?
questions.category: Protection against inversion attacks and/or model theft (MG06)

questions.text: Are there session monitoring and control systems in place to avoid reverse engineering the model through requests?
questions.category: Protection against inversion attacks and/or model theft (MG06)

questions.text: Does the system incorporate mechanisms that allow detecting attempts to evade data entry or manipulation, such as detection of data outside the domain?
Have pre-processing or similar techniques been implemented to prevent these types of attacks from occurring?
Have specific controls been developed and documented to prevent jailbreaking, such as a firewall for requests to systems?
Does the system have verification mechanisms that block attempts to bypass the restrictions or return the content of the system prompt?
Has the effectiveness of these measures been tested through drills or red team exercises?
Have vulnerabilities related to the possibility of inferring data participation in the training set been assessed?
Have privacy protection techniques, such as the use of differential privacy, been implemented to minimize these risks?
Have all the functions or tools that the intelligent system activates that could be useful to an attacker been mapped? Example: Python3 console, process management, online searches, etc.
Does the system depend on libraries not controlled by the development team?
In the work of human supervision or post-market surveillance, can the intelligent system be affected by some type of action related to social engineering?
How will secure and reliable communication between customers and those responsible for surveillance be ensured?
